• Series circuit including battery/cell and switch • Ammeter in series with motor and voltmeter in parallel with motor only (1) (1) Example of circuit • Clamp/fix the metre rule in position • Ensure the metre rule is vertical using a set square Or Ensure the metre rule is perpendicular to the floor using a set square • Place the metre rule close to the mass Or Read the height from bottom of the mass Or Attach a marker to the mass/string • Take measurements perpendicular to the scale Or Use a set square to read off the scale (1) (1) (1) (1) Accept spirit level or plumb line. Accept parallel to mass/string. Do not accept “straight” for vertical. Accept close to string/object not height. Do not allow “next to”. Do not accept “horizontally” or “parallel” for perpendicular. • There will be variation in the measurements • So repeat measurements (and calculating a mean) reduces ( the effect of) random errors. (1) (1) Do not accept eliminate/avoid. Do not accept “reduces systematic error and random error”

EITHER • Power of motor = VI • Power of lifting mass = mgh / t Or power of lifting mass = Fv with F=mg and v=h/t • So efficiency = power of lifting mass / power of lifting motor Or efficiency = (mgh / t) / VI OR • Energy transferred to motor = VIt • Energy transferred to lifting mass = mgh • So efficiency = energy transferred to lifting mass / energy transferred to motor Or efficiency = (mgh) / (VIt) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Accept definitions related to power Allow 10 as substitute for t Accept quantities as defined Must be ratio of 2 powers Allow definitions related to energy, e.g. work Allow t = 10 Allow gravitational potential energy. Accept quantities as defined Must be ratio of 2 energies Total for question 3 11 Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark
